mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of INDPRO and RSAFS changes fails to predict 60-day forward returns (multivariate IC < 0.04), or SPX returns are uncorrelated with the interaction term across a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, rising consumer credit balances indicate unsustainable debt-fueled demand that will soon reverse, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and RSAFS rises simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a rolling 2-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no positive correlation with 60-day forward equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or earnings revisions fail to track GDP momentum over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 for two consecutive months, it signals an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The combined payroll signal fails to precede negative equity returns in > 50% of historical instances, or forward returns remain positive despite the trigger activation across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets where the unemployment rate stays below 4.0%, a sustained decline in industrial production signals capacity constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when INDPRO falls during sub-4.0% unemployment periods, or the conditional forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew (t-stat > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in housing starts and a rapid increase in the federal funds rate indicate a credit-fueled housing boom colliding with tightening liquidity, which historically precedes a sharp equity drawdown over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and FEDFUNDS fails to predict 60-day forward returns (multivariate IC < 0.03), or SPX returns remain positive during concurrent housing and rate spikes across a 3-year out-of-sample test.
